title: UC1 — Mission control's roster won't come online
description: Today's mission for the Artemis pad shift: bring mission-control's on-shift roster online, then hand the diagnosis to k8s agent through the kagent dashboard chat.

steps.title: Mission setup — bring today's roster online
steps.explanation: Mission control is bringing today's on-shift roster online for the Artemis pad shift. Apply the bundle below to your vCluster: namespace, service, and deployment. Once the apply completes, the roster should be reachable on the cluster. The `kubectl apply -f` command below sends them to the cluster. The diagnostic agent you will talk to later in this tour is `k8s-agent`, kagent's built-in generic Kubernetes troubleshooter; it is already running in the cluster as part of kagent's demo profile, no extra deploy needed.

steps.title: Mission status check — is the roster live?
steps.explanation:
Mission control should be online by now. Today's pad-shift roster depends on it: every crew member opening their dashboard at shift start hits the `mission-control` deployment to pull their schedule.

Give the kubelet ~30 s to settle, then run the command below to check the roster pod's state. The pod should be `Running` and `Ready` if the deployment is healthy.

steps.title: Call the agent for help
steps.explanation:
The roster is stuck and the pad shift starts soon. Hand the diagnosis to **`k8s-agent`** through the kagent dashboard. `k8s-agent` is the generic Kubernetes troubleshooting agent that ships with kagent's demo profile, already running in the `kagent` namespace.

Use the **dashboard** button in this workshop-tour extension to open the kagent web dashboard in your browser. In the dashboard, open the chat for **`k8s-agent`** (namespace `kagent`) and walk through the two prompts below in order.

**Prompt 1: diagnose.** Paste this and read the agent's synthesis:

> In the `artemis-uc1` namespace, what is failing?

**Prompt 2: ask for the remediation command.** Once you have the root cause, ask the agent for the exact kubectl fix. Paste:

> what kubectl command to excute to fix this.

steps.title: Mission recovery — roll the roster forward to the published tag
steps.explanation:
The agent in Beat 3 named the bad tag and proposed the exact `kubectl set image` command. Run it now to update the Deployment in place: it rolls `mission-control` forward to its most recent published tag, `v1.0.0`. The kubelet will pull the new image and bring the roster online within ~30 s.

When the command completes, re-run `kubectl get pods -n artemis-uc1` if you want to watch the Pod transition out of the stuck state into `Running`.

commands.label: Roll the Deployment forward to v1.0.0
